title: National Freight Visibility Platform
entry_mechanism: Saudi logistics tech company building national freight visibility platform with Mawani and CITC as regulatory anchors
executive_summary:
Freight visibility — knowing where cargo is, when it will arrive, and what condition it is in throughout the supply chain — is the foundational digital infrastructure for modern logistics. Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 logistics hub ambition (targeting a Logistics Performance Index top-10 ranking) requires world-class freight visibility, but Saudi Arabia's current freight tracking is fragmented: each port, trucking company, and customs system operates its own data silo with no national visibility layer.

Node LG-2 targets a Saudi National Freight Visibility Platform: an integrated data layer that combines port data (Mawani), customs data (Zakat, Tax and Customs Authority), trucking GPS data, and railway data (SAR) into a single Saudi freight visibility platform. The platform serves importers, exporters, 3PLs, and government agencies — providing end-to-end Saudi supply chain visibility with Saudi data sovereignty.

The global freight visibility market was valued at $12.4 billion in 2024 growing at 28.3% CAGR. A Saudi national platform anchored by government data mandates would have immediate adoption from the 300,000+ businesses engaged in Saudi trade.

companies.name: project44
companies.rationale: project44 is the global freight visibility leader with carrier and shipper network. Saudi platform development on p44 technology.

companies.name: FourKites
companies.rationale: FourKites provides real-time supply chain visibility with active Middle East logistics customer relationships.

companies.name: Descartes Systems
companies.rationale: Descartes provides logistics management and visibility solutions with customs data integration capability.

companies.name: SAP Logistics
companies.rationale: SAP has major Saudi enterprise customer base and supply chain visibility modules. Saudi visibility platform on SAP infrastructure.

companies.name: Oracle Logistics
companies.rationale: Oracle TMS has active Saudi logistics market deployments.
companies.contact: Oracle VP Supply Chain, Saudi Arabia

partners.name: Saudi Ports Authority (Mawani)
partners.current: Port management systems; some cargo tracking; Mawani data silo
partners.upgrade: Mawani as anchor data provider; API integration between Mawani and national visibility platform; Mawani mandate for carrier reporting through national platform

partners.name: ZATCA (Customs Authority)
partners.current: Customs clearance data; cargo manifests; import/export data
partners.upgrade: ZATCA clearance data feed to national visibility platform; e-customs integration; Saudi trade data as public infrastructure

partners.name: Saudi Logistics Hub Programme / NTDP
partners.upgrade: National freight visibility as Vision 2030 logistics KPI infrastructure; mandate for all registered carriers to report through platform; Logistics Performance Index improvement programme

partners.name: Elm Company
partners.current: Government digital platforms; Absher identity infrastructure
partners.upgrade: National freight visibility platform development and operation; integration with Absher business identity for Saudi company cargo tracking
